strategy.text:
; Mechanic

Sylargh and each monster will automatically cast the spell Necromagic each turn. It has no effect until a monster dies. Then, the last monster to have died is revived on a range of 6 on the right of the monster. If that cell is unavaible, the priority changes clockwise until a valid cell on a range of 6 is found. The revived monsters are summons of the reviver and skip their first turn except for the automatic spell Necromagic. Thus, if all monsters are dead besides one, all dead monsters get revived by that one monster, in a chain from last to first. Revived monsters are unmovable for 1 turn and gain a 300 damage buff for 2 turns (actually only one as a result!). The monsters are revived with 10% of their max. HP. Erosion before the first kill is ignored. Afterwards, erosion is taken into account.

; What happens when a zombie dies?

A zombie that dies always casts the automatic spell Zombaby before its death which has 4 effects. First of all, all characters whose current HP is 90% or higher are put in the pacifist state. Secondly, the zombie inflicts fire damage in an area of 5 cells around it, both on allies and enemies. The damage is roughly 1000 in the center and gets lower when going away from the center, about 300 on the border of the AoE. The spell also instakills all other zombies that receive the fire damage. strategy.text:
• Klime starts Invulnerable. 
• To make him vulnerable, push an ally, monster, or summon into him. This invulnerability can not be removed until the start of turn 3.
• Through the Carcassacre effect, Klime and the monsters generate a white glyph on the cell they started their turn. These glyphs last for 2 turns, and a monster finishing its turn on a glyph gains 300 damage for 1 turn.
• All characters must end their turn on a white glyph, or Klime will put them in a Pacifist state for 1 turn.
• It is possible to delay this mechanic by killing all monsters before their turn.
----
Guild Expedition:
Daring Expedition

When an enemy takes damage, they swap places with their attacker and gain 5% final damage for the rest of the fight. This can only be triggered 10 times per turn per enemy.
----
Bravery Expedition

If failing to end a turn in the white glyph, Klime will now instantly kill the character at the start of their next turn.
Category:Manual spells

strategy.text:
Missiz Freezz, as well as all the monsters in the room, are Invulnerable and Heavy.

At the start of a character's turn, a black 'repelling' trap of 1-cell wide cross is placed underneath that character, with the character in the centre. Summons do not create traps. When activated, the following effects happen:
• Heals the character for 15% of their maximum HP
• Pushes the character back 1 cell
• Pushes back all entities linear (up to 6 cells) and diagonal (up to 3 cells) to the character away by one cell. This removes the Invulnerable state for one turn.
• The trap disappears
• If invisible, reveal the character
To make a monster vulnerable, they will have to be pushed back through this mechanic. They do not have to move for this mechanic. They can be made vulnerable even when already against a wall.

Any pushback damage done to a character will deal 600-800 Neutral damage and 20%Erosion per damage taken. Once Missiz Freezz is dead, the neutral damage will disappear, but the erosion remains.

Traps stay on the field until they are activated. Pushback can stack if a character hasn't moved and not activated their previous trap. A character is pushed an X amount of cells depending on how many traps are activated.
• Monsters can activate these traps and receive the 15% heals, as well as creating secondary pushback effects that could deal massive amount of pushback damage to characters.

When taking damage, Missiz Freezz can cast a spell that attracts all entities within 6 range

strategy.text:
Triggers for Pacifist State and Instakill
• If you end your turn next to an ally (summons do not count), that ally will be one shot killed. (Alchaline Solution is the name of the spell that does this)
• On turn 3 Nileza will apply -200 Dodge to all allies for 2 turns, and if allies are more than 11 cells away from Nileza at the start of his turn, they will receive the 'Pacifist' state for 2 turns.
• On turn 5 Nileza will apply -200 Lock to all allies for 2 turns, and if allies are more than 9 cells away from Nileza at the start of his turn, they will receive the 'Pacifist' state for 2 turns.
• On turn 7 Nileza will apply the 'Gravity' state to all allies for 2 turns, and if allies are more than 7 cells away from Nileza at the start of his turn, they will receive the 'Pacifist' state for 2 turns.
• On turn 9 and every other turn thereafter (turns 9, 11, 13 etc) Nileza will apply the 'Unhealable' state to all allies for 2 turns, and if allies are more than 5 cells away from Nileza at the start of his turn, they will receive the 'Pacifist' state for 2 turns (both effects are renewable every 2 turns unless Nileza is dead).
• As of update 2.42 Nileza is no longer invulnerable.

He also has an ability which causes you to swap places with Nileza when attacking him from more than one range away with any attack. When he switches places with the attacker, he inflicts twice the damage received (or that he would have received if vulnerable) as air damage including 40% erosion, in an AoE

strategy.text:
Merkator and all monsters in the fight are Unmovable. At the start of their turn, every monster casts Sea Yousoun, which has three effects:
• Characters and/or summons in melee range of the monster are instantly killed
• Merkator does not have this effect
• Enemies in melee range receive around 700 water damage and enter the Small Fry state for 2 turns
• All entities at exactly 2 cells away from the monster are healed by 20% of their max HP

Any attempt to remove MP from enemies inflicts 500 Fire steal.

Moving enemies is not necessary for the fight, but if need be:
• Hitting a monster in melee will pushback all entities linear and diagonally to that monster
• Hitting a monster in the Small Fry state in melee will attract all linear and diagonal instead
----
Guild Expedition:
Daring Expedition

Excluding Merkator, all monsters in the fight share damage. Every time a monster suffers damage, it and the character who dealt it will infinitely gain 2% final Damage.
----
Bravery Expedition

Merkator is Invulnerable. Every time he is made vulnerable, he infinitely gains 1 MP.
• There are two ways to make him vulnerable until the start of his next turn, which will require moving him. That can be done through the Sea An Enemy effect:
• He must be in a diagonal of a 3x3 diagonal cross within a character and take damage from a weapon
• He must be linear within a 3x3 diagonal cross around a monster, and the monster takes melee damage
Category:Manual spells
Category:Sunktuary

strategy.text:
; The Pouch
At the start of the fight, King Nidas casts Nidas touch which makes him immune to the spell bribery and makes him unmovable for the first three turns. In addition, a random character on your team will turn gold and summon a pouch. The pouch is immune to the spells immunity, spell rebound, natural defense, and repercussion, and has 3,000 hitpoints and 2 mp. Once the pouch dies, it will be summoned by the enemy closest to your team with 5000 HP. If you kill it again, a random character summons it. So basically, it always switches sides when killed. The pouch is summoned next to the character on top, then priority switches to the right.

The pouch is an important mechanic during the fight which works differently, depending on whose side the pouch is currently summoned.

When the Pouch belongs to your team: On the pouch's turn, it will cast a spell which instantly kills any summons within two cells linear to it, reduces the duration of all effects on the pouch by two turns, and heals your team by 10% of their maximum vitality. It's in heavy state and unhealable state!

When it belongs to the enemies: It applies a 2 MP buff to all enemies each turn, so they basically play with + 2MP continuously, as long as they have the pouch.

At the beginning of each player's turn a red Bonbomb is summoned exactly 2 cells northeast from the player, unless the cell is occupied or unavailable, in which case it will be summoned 2 cells east from the player, and so on clockwise until it is finally summoned. If all cells within the 2 range area are occupied, the Bonbomb will be 3 cells away, following the same pattern. Bonbombs are considered to be allies, and are completely invulnerable. They can be pushed or pulled, but they cannot be picked up by Pandawas or moved through Eliotrope portals.

Bonbombs will inevitably explode 1 turn after being summoned, at the end of the player's turn, and there is simply no way to avoid this. All red Bonbombs that are diagonal to any Bonbomb that explodes will turn into a white Bonbomb. When red Bonbombs explode they will instantly kill any allies linear to them, within 10 cells from the bomb. When white Bonbombs explode they will just about fully heal any allies linear to them and remove the Queen's "Impending Death" state, within 10 cells from the bomb. Both red and white Bonbombs also deal 1,000 fire damage to any enemy (except the Queen) linear to them, also within 10 cells from the bomb. The Queen is totally immune to the effects of Bonbombs. If a player dies, their summoned Bonbombs will immediately explode. Bonbombs do not activate the Terristocrat's Bomb Ilical, and vice versa.

strategy.text:
Protoz'orror is Invulnerable. Throughout the fight, it is Unmovable.
• The only way to move it, is by damaging it while standing linear to it. That will attract Protoz'orror in melee range. 
• This effect only activates on the very first hit received after its turn.
• Protoz'orror can be attracted linearly until the first non-linear hit.

Each time a character is hit in the fight, a purple glyph is generated on the cell they are standing on for 1 turn. Glyphs can be stacked and will separately deal the damage.
• Entering the glyph in any way will deal Air damage. 
• It is possible to generate a glyph on your own by hitting yourself on the desired cell.
• The glyph will stop any type of movement.

To make Protoz'orror vulnerable, it has to have been in the Infection state for 10 turns in a row. This state is applied when it is attracted into a purple glyph. This state will last for three turns, meaning it will need to be put in a purple glyph every second turn.
• It is possible to attract him three turns later, so long as the last character before Protoz'orror's turn moves it into the glyph.
• If the effect is lost at any time, it will have to be re-applied and the counter restarts.

While under the Infection state, Protoz'orror will turn purple, gain 2 MP, and change the properties of all its spells. It loses all its Power and base damage buffs.

• Protoz'orror can only hit in linear range

; The Aurour

Imagine it as a clock hand because there are 12 fixed positions on the border of the map which it rotates through (not to mention the map is somewhat shaped like a big clock). If one of the fixed positions is occupied by any entity, the Aurour will always change places with it when rotating. The position determines the current state of the Aurour, for example "Twelfth hour" state when it's located on the top of the map or "Sixth hour" state when it's on the bottom of the map. strategy.text:
; The boss

Catseye is invulnerable until turn 21. He deals tons of damage, but messes up your placement unpredictably which renders the fight insanely difficult if you don't exclude him from the fight during the waves. The easiest option would be to use a Steamer to imprison Catseye in one of the bottom corners of the map by placing 2 towers around him and the third one behind one of the towers. Towers cannot swap with Catseye, so he can only push them. That's why you must always have Salt Armor on the Tower that Catseye can push away! The sufficient cooldown to do this requires a class item. A Steamer is not obligatory however: any composition could keep Catseye in one of the bottom corners with a summon (like a Pandawasta) or even a character that does nothing else. Lock is unneeded since Catseye has 300 dodge anyway. This would require that the fight takes place 13+ cells away from Catseye for the entire time of defeating the waves! Otherwise, Catseye will run away from the summon/character to cooperate with something if he can, instead of staying next to the summon/character in the corner all the time.

Once Catseye is vulnerable (turn 21), he gains 300 MP resistance infinitely and a state on top so that he can no longer be pushed or pulled. You can still throw him.

strategy.text:
Captain Meno starts the fight with a significant amount of HP and x50% final damage sustained.
• His allies start the fight in an invulnerable state and a limited lifespan of 3 turns:
• The first turn they are alive, they have +1 MP
• The second turn they are alive, they have -1 MP
• After the third turn, they die

Each turn:
• Captain Meno will summon 3 additional allies (or more, if there are 5 or more players in the fight), so by the third turn you can expect to have a total of 9 additional enemies on the map at any given time.
• Captain Meno will apply a pink glyph under each player character or player character's summon. 
• Any player who is on a glyph at the start of '''Meno's turn (not counting the turn he creates the glyph) gains 1,000 shield points for 2 turns. 
• They will also deal 500-1000 fire damage to any linear ally (player and summon).
• Captain Meno will heal himself for a significant amount, upwards of 3,000 Vitality.

strategy.text:
Koutoulou Larva is not invulnerable, but it does have a passive with crucial effects:
• Any damage dealt to an enemy will make you swap places with the nearest entity (excluding summons)
• This can not be avoided with any states and is forced no matter what. Indirect damage such as poison does not trigger this mechanic.
• Doing it twice to the same enemy in the same turn will make that enemy Invulnerable and add a stack to the Madness state
• Doing it twice to the same ally will add a stack of Madness to both of you
• Koutoulou Larva can teleport across the map to any player in line of sight. In melee range it can cast Koutonian Blow, which deals quite some damage, as well as adding to the stack of Madness.
• It is crucial to try to not end your turn in its line of sight. 
• Koutoulou Larva can also swap positions with its allies twice a turn.

Madness stacks and effects:
Note that these are accumulative. Every stack has all the effects that came before it, as well as the effect of its own number.
• Stack 2+: 100% damage copied to allies within 2 cells of you
• Stack 5+: Heals received x50%
• Stack 6+: Damage sustained x100%
• Stack 10: Instant death

Every passing turn will reduce a character's stack by 1.

Most monsters in the fight have the ability to create a clone of characters. Every clone will rush towards the enemy. Damage dealt to a clone is dealt to the character, as well. 
• To kill the clone, it has to be pushed into an enemy. This will deal no damage to the 'owne

strategy.text:
At the start of the fight, Dantinea summons a Colosshell for every character in the fight. The associated shell is denoted by numerals above the shell and above the characters.
• At the end of every round where your Colosshell is still alive , you will lose 1 MP and inflict only 50% damage.
• On the sixth turn Dantinea will re-summon the dead shells. Any alive characters whose shells are not yet dead, will be instantly killed. 
It is recommended to focus on killing your own shell before helping others.
----
Guild Expedition:
Daring Expedition

The Colosshells are Invulnerable. They can only be killed by finishing your turn next to the Colosshell assigned to you. Note that any Colosshells summoned from round six on will be vulnerable and killable like normal.
----
Bravery Expedition

The Colosshells have +50% Vitality and share their damage with each other. Dantinea also infinitely gains +100 Power at the start of her turn.
• Note that, due to the damage sharing abilities, it is possible for Dantinea to kill everybody whose Colosshell is still alive at the start of round six.

Category:Manual spells

strategy.text:
General room mechanics

In addition to the effect common to all monsters within the pyramid, there are a couple additional effects that are new to the final room. First and foremost, all mobs within the fight will have a revive mechanic similar to the one seen in Sylargh. Monsters killed within the fight will revive immediately after the player that killed the mob ends their turn. After being killed, the revived mob will skip their following turn. When revived, the mob will spawn a glyph on their location that can be used to remove Tal Kasha invulnerability. Additionally, the first time a mob is revived and every instance thereafter, the mob will revive with half it's base hp and -1 MP permanently (Non-stackable). It's important to note that the hp of the revived monster can be further reduced with erosion.

While stripping the mp of enemies can be immensely useful for this fight, doing so to Tal Kasha must be done with caution as any attempt to take mp from her will make it so that the caster swaps positions with her. This swap can happen only once per turn.

At the beginning of Tal Kasha's turn, she is capable of channeling her own spells through other mobs on the map. Typically this will include a linear pull and a high damage melee nuke that can hit around 1800 neutral damage. As a result, it is highly recommended that you avoid ending in a position where you will be linear to an enemy (including Tal Kasha) when Tal Kasha begins her turn.

strategy.text:
At the start of the fight Anerice casts Vampyric Resilience. It reduces her damage sustained by 90%. (infinite)

In short the goal is to increase damage sustained by boss. She can transform your allies (summons too) into Ghouls and every existing Ghoul increases damage she takes. Now to details:
• Ghouls have increased damage sustained by 150%.
• Each Ghoul present on the field increases '''Anerice's damage sustained by 30%.'''
• Anerice can teleport Ghouls to herself. Its done by Cooperation.
• Ghouls can be boosted by her Bloodthirsty Appetite spell. 
• You get out of the Ghoul state by hitting yourself or being hit by ally.

Thus for each ghoul present on the map, the damage suffered by Anerice will increase and from 3 ghouls present you will inflict “normal” damage, the ideal being to have 4+ entities transformed into ghouls in order to kill her quickly.

strategy.text:
Solar is Invulnerable. He can push you infinitely back if you are further than 6 cells away from him at the start of his turn. His mechanic centers around cycling through four different phases: Aurora, Zenith, Nightfall, and Nadir. Every cycle gives him different spells, as well as different effects for the entities in the fight. A glyph is also placed in the corners of the map, the effect of which depend on the state Solar is in.
• Turn 1-3: Aurora
• Returns HP to him and his monsters, +1000 AP/MP Parry. +1 AP for players. Solar can be pushed, but not lifted. His damage is moderate, and he can shield allies and attack in area of effect.
• Glyph: -300 Power
• Turn 4-6: Zenith
• Boosts all monsters with 200 Power and removes -6 Range for 1 turn from players. Players gain 2 AP. Solar deals high damage and can teleport. 
• Glyph: +20% Erosion
• Turn 7-9: Nightfall'
• Removes -1 AP for players. His damage is low. He is Unlockable.
• Glyph: -4 Range
• Turn 10-12: Nadir
• He is vulnerable. He can be pushed and/or lifted in this state. His damage is moderate.
• Glyph: -2 AP

It is possible to affect Solar's states directly by using Necronyx on him. It will change his state in this succession: Aurora > Zenith > Twilight > Nadir.

It is only possible to deal damage to Solar in the Nadir state. If he does not die in three turns, the cycle will restart. The process can be accelerated through Necronyx.
• NOTE: Necronyx must be cast on Solar before killing him, or the fight will be lost.

strategy.text:
Dazahk Freezammer is practically invulnerable through 100%+ elemental resistances, until he has reached State 3 of Stubbygerness. He starts at State 1, and every round he can increase his state so long as he deals no damage to an enemy (summons not included).

Once he is in State 3, at the start of his turn, the following can be done to make him vulnerable:
• When pushed or pulled he permanently loses -5% resistance for the duration of the fight
• If receiving pushback damage, he will permanently gain +10% resistance
• State 3 also gives him +3 MP and +150% for two turns.
Note that Gravity does not stop him from teleporting.
----
Guild Expedition:
Daring Expedition

When a monster increases their Stubbygerness state (by not having hit an enemy in their previous turn), they will get the following bonus:
• +3 MP 
• +3 AP 
• 300 Power
• If their state does not change, they will not regain the bonus of their current state. It may therefore be useful to allow one or multiple enemies to damage a character to keep them in a beneficial state.

When a character attacks any monster, they will also gain the bonus related to the state that monster is in. This lasts for one turn. Bonuses can not be combined, and only one can be had at the same time.
----
Bravery Expedition

Each time a monster takes damage, Dazahk Freezammer switches places with them.
Category:Manual spells

strategy.text:
To remove her invulnerability you need to kill 3 Chokao Chips (its death from being resummoned by Chocomancers does not count).

The Chip kill count is indicated by Roman numerals: I, II, III. At the state "III" she loses her 'Invulnerable' state forever (however she can still be temporary invulnerable because of Chokao Chip's passive spell if she is its current summoner).

Each time a Chip is killed (not by being resummoned) she gains % final damage infinitely (triggered 3 times: 52%, 74% or 99% final damage bonus respectively).

It's recommended to use high damage or poison classes like Iops or Srams. Leave the boss for last and try to avoid damaging the Chip until then.
Category:Manual spells

strategy.text:
• Avoid being in line of sight of any of the monsters, or if you have to be in line of sight, stay within 4 range or less of them so that they do not enter the Ferocity of the Protectors state.
• Use summons to block line of sight but make sure to not wall off you or your allies since you still need to be able to attack the monsters too. Also, do not stand in a line with your summons since if the Wallopoppy casts Watery Fist on the summon it will attract you toward the summon which could mess up your positioning.
• Focus on the Camelliaxe first since it can teleport. This can be countered bringing a character that can apply the Gravity state to the Camelliaxe.
• Bring a character with high Lock. This will make sure that the monsters do not enter the Ferocity of the Protectors state since they will be within 4 range or less of you or your allies. Be careful of the Soldalia who can apply the Rooted state and the Camelliaxe who can teleport as that can counter any amount of Lock.
• Make sure to use an unbewitching spell if the Arm-of-the-Valley casts Thorny Sharing. This spell allows the Arm-of-the-Valley to gain vitality every time the target takes damage.

To read more strategies check out the [https://www.dofuspourlesnoobs.com/chambre-des-malefices.html#strategie-globale Dofus pour les Noobs guide].

strategy.text:
Royal Tofu has no mechanics and can be freely damaged and killed.

----
Guild Expedition:
Normal expedition

Royal Tofu is Invulnerable. 
To make it vulnerable, it must not use any MP for two turns in a row. This can be done in two ways:
• Lock or trap Royal Tofu so it can not move.
• Every time any monster is hit in melee range, it will permanently remove 1 MP from it for the rest of the fight. This can be done 5 times per turn. When the MP is low enough, it can be safely locked, or its MP can be reduced to 0 to prevent its moving at all.

Every two turns, all monsters receive a buff that lasts for 1 turn:
• Turn 2 - +50% Final Damage.
• Turn 4 - Royal Tofu receives shield points equal to 10% of its HP.
• Turn 6 - Invulnerable for one turn.
• Turn 8 - Heals for 10% of its HP.
• Turn 10 - Repeats the buffs starting with Turn 2.

Every turn, Royal Tofu places a black glyph of a 4-cell circle that lasts two turns. For monsters, this glyph reduces ranged damage taken by -20%. It also heals them for 8% of their maximum HP.

strategy.text:
Smiths' Chest has no mechanics and can be freely damaged and killed.

Guild Expedition:
Normal expedition
• Smiths' Chest will remain Invulnerable until its first turn after all other monsters are killed.
• If you kill a monster, Smiths' Chest will immediately teleport next to you and instantly kill you. 
• To avoid this mechanic, keep a monster at 10% HP or less. At the start of Smiths' Chest's turn, it will kill any monster at or below that threshold.
• The same mechanic counts for player characters as well. If a player has hit that threshold, Smiths' Chest will kill them at the start of its turn.
• Every time a monster is killed, the Smiths' Chest gains +1 MP, +5% Final Damage, and visually gets 10% larger.

strategy.text:
Crocabulia has no mechanics and can be freely damaged and killed.
----
Guild Expedition:
Normal Expedition

Crocabulia is Invulnerable. Every turn she will summon a Shell, up to 10 cells away. This has 55,000 HP and 8MP, but there is no need to kill it. 

At the start of Crocabulias turn, she will instantly kill all summoned Shells that are linear and diagonal from her at an infinite range. Any Shells within 3 cells of an exploding one will also explode.

Any entities (monsters AND players) within 3 cells of a Shell when it explodes will experience the following:
• Monsters are fully healed
• Characters or summons are instantly killed 

To make Crocabulia vulnerable, she needs to have exploded three shells directly by herself. Chained explosions do not count.

This means at least three Shells must have been put in linear or diagonal lines from her and been exploded by her start-of-turn mechanic. Note that the Shells do not have to have been killed within the same turn. As long as she's exploded at least three during the fight, she will become vulnerable.

----
Guild Expedition:
Normal expedition

Legendary Crackler has a buff that gives:
• +50% all resistances, +200 flat damage for all elements, and +500 AP and MP Parry.

When he is hit with a neutral element, all of its allies will gain 3,000 shield points for two turns.

NOTE: Legendary Crackler will cast Dazing Stone when it has 20 AP. This will deal massive amounts of damage, so it is recommended to prevent him from being able to cast it.

The other monsters:
• +20% all resistances, +100 flat damage for all elements, and +200 AP and MP 

At the end of every monster's turn, they place a black glyph on their cell. If a character enters this glyph, they enter the Crackling state:
• Gives 30% of your HP as shield, -100 MP, and +100% Damage received for the rest of the fight
This state allows for characters to debuff the enemies, as well as removing the state from themselves, in two ways:
• When taking melee damage, all monsters adjacent to that character lose their buff for three turns.
• When taking pushback damage, all monsters in a 3 by 3 square around that character lose their buff for two turns.

Normal Expedition

Koolich has 100% resistances in all elements. Every four turns he can turn invisible.

Damaging Koolich will turn entities (both monsters and characters) into Gobkools when:
• On even turns they are linear with Koolich 
• On odd turns they are diagonal with Koolich
This effect lasts for one turn. 

If Koolich suffers more damage, any entities already in the Gobkool state in-line with him (linear on even turns, diagonal on odd), will instantly die.
• Any Cave Gobballs in-line with Koolich will not die, but instead gain +3AP and MP, +100% final Damage, 10% of its maximum HP healed per turn, as well as gaining a new spell.

• Multiple Gobkool's will need to be placed within 2 cells of Koolich to enable a substantial amount of damage to be done. Keep in mind that transformed characters will instantly die if they are still in-line with Koolich when he takes damage.

strategy.text:
Sponge Mob has no mechanics and can be freely damaged and killed.
----
Guild Expedition:
Normal Expedition

Sponge Mob has 100% resistance, and all the monsters in the fight are Invulnerable. Killing Sponge Mob will finish the fight, the other monsters do not need to be killed.

To make Sponge Mob vulnerable, hitting any of the Snappers will remove -1% resistance from Sponge Mob and deal 100% of the damage to Sponge Mob. Every Snapper has its own element it will remove from Sponge Mob:
• Brown Snapper - Fire
• Blue Snapper - Water
• Green Snapper - Earth
• White Snapper - Air
It does not matter what element they are hit with. Every hit = -1% resistance in their element. Sponge Mob will lose resistance no matter where he is on the map. Area of effect spells are therefore quite useful.

Throughout the fight, the Snappers will be fighting and placing down glyphs with various effects.

Sponge Mob can summon a Kloon Snapper, which turns invisible for one turn when it takes damage, as well as be pushed back 2-6 cells. When killed, it will join the characters' side. Whoever killed it will control it, which allows for more damage to be done to the Snappers and thus Sponge Mob. 
• When a monster kills this allied Kloon Snapper, it will join their side and be controlled by that monster. Sponge Mob will then summon another.
• Note that this mechanic is not needed to win the fight.

• When a monster kills this allied Kloon Snapper, it will join their side and be controlled by that monster. Sponge Mob will then summon another.
• Note that this mechanic is not needed to win the fight.

strategy.text:
Golden Scarabugly has no mechanics and can be freely damaged and killed.
----
Guild Expedition:
Normal Expedition

Golden Scarabugly has 100% resistance to all elements except neutral, which is at 49%.

To remove a resistance, the Scaraleaf of the corresponding element has to be killed. When it dies, Golden Scarabugly loses 50% of its resistance in that element. It will also boost the Golden Scarabugly +1 MP, 100 Pushback damage, and add the corresponding elemental boost, as well as deal poison damage in its element to the killer (3 turns).
• White Scaraleaf - Air - 400 Agility
• Green Scaraleaf - Earth - 400 Strength
• Blue Scaraleaf - Water - 400 Chance
• Red Scaraleaf - Fire - 400 Intelligence
• Black Scaraleaf - N/A - 250 Air, Earth, Fire, Water damage
When a Scaraleaf is killed, it will summon a Spark corresponding to its element. It has 1,100 HP, and deals massive damage in close range. When a Black Scaraleaf dies, it will add 250 damage to the Golden Scarabugly.

Starting from turn one, then every five turns after, Golden Scarabugly will summon an Immature Scaraleaf. After two turns, it will mature into a random Scaraleaf.
• If it transforms into a Scaraleaf that has already died, Golden Scarabugly will regain the resistances it had lost.
• If there are no other Scaraleafs in the fight, it will transform into a Black Scaraleaf instead.

strategy.text:
• Klime starts Invulnerable. 
• To make him vulnerable, push an ally, monster, or summon into him. This invulnerability can not be removed until the start of turn 3.
• Through the Carcassacre effect, Klime and the monsters generate a white glyph on the cell they started their turn. These glyphs last for 2 turns, and a monster finishing its turn on a glyph gains 300 damage for 1 turn.
• All characters must end their turn on a white glyph, or Klime will put them in a Pacifist state for 1 turn.
• It is possible to delay this mechanic by killing all monsters before their turn.
----
Guild Expedition:
Daring Expedition

When an enemy takes damage, they swap places with their attacker and gain 5% final damage for the rest of the fight. This can only be triggered 10 times per turn per enemy.
----
Bravery Expedition

If failing to end a turn in the white glyph, Klime will now instantly kill the character at the start of their next turn.

name: Expédition de l'Audace - Forgefroide de Missiz Frizz

strategy.text:
Missiz Freezz, as well as all the monsters in the room, are Invulnerable and Heavy.

At the start of a character's turn, a black 'repelling' trap of 1-cell wide cross is placed underneath that character, with the character in the centre. Summons do not create traps. When activated, the following effects happen:
• Heals the character for 15% of their maximum HP
• Pushes the character back 1 cell
• Pushes back all entities linear (up to 6 cells) and diagonal (up to 3 cells) to the character away by one cell. This removes the Invulnerable state for one turn.
• The trap disappears
• If invisible, reveal the character
To make a monster vulnerable, they will have to be pushed back through this mechanic. They do not have to move for this mechanic. They can be made vulnerable even when already against a wall.

Any pushback damage done to a character will deal 600-800 Neutral damage and 20%Erosion per damage taken. Once Missiz Freezz is dead, the neutral damage will disappear, but the erosion remains.

Traps stay on the field until they are activated. Pushback can stack if a character hasn't moved and not activated their previous trap. A character is pushed an X amount of cells depending on how many traps are activated.
• Monsters can activate these traps and receive the 15% heals, as well as creating secondary pushback effects that could deal massive amount of pushback damage to characters.

When taking damage, Missiz Freezz can cast a spell that attracts all entities within 6 range

name: Expédition de l'Audace - Donjon du Comte Harebourg

strategy.text:
Count Harebourg is Invulnerable throughout the fight.

At the start of your turn, you will be assigned a rotation degree. 
• Every single spell you want to aim at a specific cell, will instead have to be aimed to your rotation cell in order to be cast in the desired cell. 
• If you hit a monster while next to it, each time you hit it you will have to rotate your attacks an additional 90 degrees anti-clockwise from the original position

Once you get the rotations down, here's the easiest strategy for the fight (for 4 loot):
Save Count Harebourg for last, and don't pay too much attention to him. He has lots of range, lots of MP, and no LOS required. He will most likely always be able to hit you. Throw him away if he gets close to you, but don't go out of your way to do so.
• Cycloid
• Locks you and unmovable.
• Treadfast 
• Moves characters around and can mess up positions. Steals HP from characters and enemies in range (though not in close combat) time you hit him. The stealing is unbewitchable.
• Nocturnowl
• Latches onto you and attracts himself (+ applies the Unhealable state) if you are linear. He only has 3 MP. Hitting him in close combat will reflect all damage.
• Bubotron
• Applies spells throughout the fight, but is otherwise passive unless close to a person. At low HP it hits very highly.
• Count Harebourg
• Places a glyph around him every turn. This glyph has a range of 3 linear to where he starts his turn. If you start your turn on it (not end), you die. Do not end

name: Expédition de l'Audace - Aquadôme de Merkator

strategy.text:
Merkator and all monsters in the fight are Unmovable. At the start of their turn, every monster casts Sea Yousoun, which has three effects:
• Characters and/or summons in melee range of the monster are instantly killed
• Merkator does not have this effect
• Enemies in melee range receive around 700 water damage and enter the Small Fry state for 2 turns
• All entities at exactly 2 cells away from the monster are healed by 20% of their max HP

Any attempt to remove MP from enemies inflicts 500 Fire steal.

Moving enemies is not necessary for the fight, but if need be:
• Hitting a monster in melee will pushback all entities linear and diagonally to that monster
• Hitting a monster in the Small Fry state in melee will attract all linear and diagonal instead
----
Guild Expedition:
Daring Expedition

Excluding Merkator, all monsters in the fight share damage. Every time a monster suffers damage, it and the character who dealt it will infinitely gain 2% final Damage.
----
Bravery Expedition

Merkator is Invulnerable. Every time he is made vulnerable, he infinitely gains 1 MP.
• There are two ways to make him vulnerable until the start of his next turn, which will require moving him. That can be done through the Sea An Enemy effect:
• He must be in a diagonal of a 3x3 diagonal cross within a character and take damage from a weapon
• He must be linear within a 3x3 diagonal cross around a monster, and the monster takes melee damage
Category:Manual spells
Category:Sunktuary

strategy.text:
Protoz'orror is Invulnerable. Throughout the fight, it is Unmovable.
• The only way to move it, is by damaging it while standing linear to it. That will attract Protoz'orror in melee range. 
• This effect only activates on the very first hit received after its turn.
• Protoz'orror can be attracted linearly until the first non-linear hit.

Each time a character is hit in the fight, a purple glyph is generated on the cell they are standing on for 1 turn. Glyphs can be stacked and will separately deal the damage.
• Entering the glyph in any way will deal Air damage. 
• It is possible to generate a glyph on your own by hitting yourself on the desired cell.
• The glyph will stop any type of movement.

To make Protoz'orror vulnerable, it has to have been in the Infection state for 10 turns in a row. This state is applied when it is attracted into a purple glyph. This state will last for three turns, meaning it will need to be put in a purple glyph every second turn.
• It is possible to attract him three turns later, so long as the last character before Protoz'orror's turn moves it into the glyph.
• If the effect is lost at any time, it will have to be re-applied and the counter restarts.

While under the Infection state, Protoz'orror will turn purple, gain 2 MP, and change the properties of all its spells. It loses all its Power and base damage buffs.

• Protoz'orror can only hit in linear range
• Monsters in the room will summon a Malamoeba. This has to be killed every

strategy.text:
Koutoulou Larva is not invulnerable, but it does have a passive with crucial effects:
• Any damage dealt to an enemy will make you swap places with the nearest entity (excluding summons)
• This can not be avoided with any states and is forced no matter what. Indirect damage such as poison does not trigger this mechanic.
• Doing it twice to the same enemy in the same turn will make that enemy Invulnerable and add a stack to the Madness state
• Doing it twice to the same ally will add a stack of Madness to both of you
• Koutoulou Larva can teleport across the map to any player in line of sight. In melee range it can cast Koutonian Blow, which deals quite some damage, as well as adding to the stack of Madness.
• It is crucial to try to not end your turn in its line of sight. 
• Koutoulou Larva can also swap positions with its allies twice a turn.

Madness stacks and effects:
Note that these are accumulative. Every stack has all the effects that came before it, as well as the effect of its own number.
• Stack 2+: 100% damage copied to allies within 2 cells of you
• Stack 5+: Heals received x50%
• Stack 6+: Damage sustained x100%
• Stack 10: Instant death

Every passing turn will reduce a character's stack by 1.

Most monsters in the fight have the ability to create a clone of characters. Every clone will rush towards the enemy. Damage dealt to a clone is dealt to the character, as well. 
• To kill the clone, it has to be pushed into an enemy. This will deal no damage to the 'owne

strategy.text:
At the start of the fight, Dantinea summons a Colosshell for every character in the fight. The associated shell is denoted by numerals above the shell and above the characters.
• At the end of every round where your Colosshell is still alive , you will lose 1 MP and inflict only 50% damage.
• On the sixth turn Dantinea will re-summon the dead shells. Any alive characters whose shells are not yet dead, will be instantly killed. 
It is recommended to focus on killing your own shell before helping others.

Daring Expedition

The Colosshells are Invulnerable. They can only be killed by finishing your turn next to the Colosshell assigned to you. Note that any Colosshells summoned from round six on will be vulnerable and killable like normal.

Bravery Expedition

The Colosshells have +50% Vitality and share their damage with each other. Dantinea also infinitely gains +100 Power at the start of her turn.
• Note that, due to the damage sharing abilities, it is possible for Dantinea to kill everybody whose Colosshell is still alive at the start of round six.

name: Expédition de l'Audace - Tour de Solar

strategy.text:
Solar is Invulnerable. He can push you infinitely back if you are further than 6 cells away from him at the start of his turn. His mechanic centers around cycling through four different phases: Aurora, Zenith, Nightfall, and Nadir. Every cycle gives him different spells, as well as different effects for the entities in the fight. A glyph is also placed in the corners of the map, the effect of which depend on the state Solar is in.
• Turn 1-3: Aurora
• Returns HP to him and his monsters, +1000 AP/MP Parry. +1 AP for players. Solar can be pushed, but not lifted. His damage is moderate, and he can shield allies and attack in area of effect.
• Glyph: -300 Power
• Turn 4-6: Zenith
• Boosts all monsters with 200 Power and removes -6 Range for 1 turn from players. Players gain 2 AP. Solar deals high damage and can teleport. 
• Glyph: +20% Erosion
• Turn 7-9: Nightfall'
• Removes -1 AP for players. His damage is low. He is Unlockable.
• Glyph: -4 Range
• Turn 10-12: Nadir
• He is vulnerable. He can be pushed and/or lifted in this state. His damage is moderate.
• Glyph: -2 AP

It is possible to affect Solar's states directly by using Necronyx on him. It will change his state in this succession: Aurora > Zenith > Twilight > Nadir.

It is only possible to deal damage to Solar in the Nadir state. If he does not die in three turns, the cycle will restart. The process can be accelerated through Necronyx.
• NOTE: Necronyx must be cast on Solar before killing him, or the fight will be lost.

• Boosts all monsters with 200 Power and removes -6 Range for 1 turn from players. Players gain 2 AP. Solar deals high damage and can teleport.

• Removes -1 AP for players. His damage is low. He is Unlockable.

• He is vulnerable. He can be pushed and/or lifted in this state. His damage is moderate.

name: Expédition de l'Audace - Brasserie du roi Dazak

strategy.text:
Dazahk Freezammer is practically invulnerable through 100%+ elemental resistances, until he has reached State 3 of Stubbygerness. He starts at State 1, and every round he can increase his state so long as he deals no damage to an enemy (summons not included).

Once he is in State 3, at the start of his turn, the following can be done to make him vulnerable:
• When pushed or pulled he permanently loses -5% resistance for the duration of the fight
• If receiving pushback damage, he will permanently gain +10% resistance
• State 3 also gives him +3 MP and +150% for two turns.
Note that Gravity does not stop him from teleporting.
----
Guild Expedition:
Daring Expedition

When a monster increases their Stubbygerness state (by not having hit an enemy in their previous turn), they will get the following bonus:
• +3 MP 
• +3 AP 
• 300 Power
• If their state does not change, they will not regain the bonus of their current state. It may therefore be useful to allow one or multiple enemies to damage a character to keep them in a beneficial state.

When a character attacks any monster, they will also gain the bonus related to the state that monster is in. This lasts for one turn. Bonuses can not be combined, and only one can be had at the same time.
----
Bravery Expedition

Each time a monster takes damage, Dazahk Freezammer switches places with them.

name: Expédition de la Bravoure - Tour de Solar

strategy.text:
Solar is Invulnerable. He can push you infinitely back if you are further than 6 cells away from him at the start of his turn. His mechanic centers around cycling through four different phases: Aurora, Zenith, Nightfall, and Nadir. Every cycle gives him different spells, as well as different effects for the entities in the fight. A glyph is also placed in the corners of the map, the effect of which depend on the state Solar is in.
• Turn 1-3: Aurora
• Returns HP to him and his monsters, +1000 AP/MP Parry. +1 AP for players. Solar can be pushed, but not lifted. His damage is moderate, and he can shield allies and attack in area of effect.
• Glyph: -300 Power
• Turn 4-6: Zenith
• Boosts all monsters with 200 Power and removes -6 Range for 1 turn from players. Players gain 2 AP. Solar deals high damage and can teleport. 
• Glyph: +20% Erosion
• Turn 7-9: Nightfall'
• Removes -1 AP for players. His damage is low. He is Unlockable.
• Glyph: -4 Range
• Turn 10-12: Nadir
• He is vulnerable. He can be pushed and/or lifted in this state. His damage is moderate.
• Glyph: -2 AP

It is possible to affect Solar's states directly by using Necronyx on him. It will change his state in this succession: Aurora > Zenith > Twilight > Nadir.

It is only possible to deal damage to Solar in the Nadir state. If he does not die in three turns, the cycle will restart. The process can be accelerated through Necronyx.
• NOTE: Necronyx must be cast on Solar before killing him, or the fight will be lost.

name: Expédition de la Bravoure - Belvédère d'Ilyzaelle

strategy.text:
Ilyzaelle is not invulnerable, but increases the Vitality by 300% of all monsters, as well as herself. She has two thresholds for her health: 75%, and 50%, that will do the following when reached:
• Ilyzaelle turns friendly (the '''King's Guard state) for two turns and places a glyph. In the glyph:
• When entering, monsters immediately take 15,000 Neutral damage
• Players sustain 'x70% Damage and deal 400% Damage
• This makes life steal especially effective

When the two turns are up, Ilyzaelle becomes Invulnerable for one turn, where she visibly turns grey. After that she plays as normal, and the next threshold to hit is 50% Vitality, after which she will enter the 'King's Guard state again.

Placing Erosion or Cawwot glyphs can kill you due to the Gron state. If you have a character alive after that, she will simply end the fight two turns later.

Every three turns she will attract all players (but not summons). Be mindful of her jumping attack, since it will add 40% Erosion. Her linear attack applies the Pacifist' state.

Note that pushing, pulling, or removing the MP of the other monsters in the fight will give them an additional spell they can use on you. Displacement spells do not'' count for this mechanic. This is visible by a player being in the Burn state.
• Skoul: Puts all players in their starting position and make them sustain x150% Damage 
• Spirigneous: Reveals invisible entities
• Ectorch: Deals 707 to 840 Fire damage throughout the whole map, regardless of lin

name: Expédition de la Bravoure - Palais de Dantinéa

strategy.text:
At the start of the fight, Dantinea summons a Colosshell for every character in the fight. The associated shell is denoted by numerals above the shell and above the characters.
• At the end of every round where your Colosshell is still alive , you will lose 1 MP and inflict only 50% damage.
• On the sixth turn Dantinea will re-summon the dead shells. Any alive characters whose shells are not yet dead, will be instantly killed. 
It is recommended to focus on killing your own shell before helping others.
----
Guild Expedition:
Daring Expedition

The Colosshells are Invulnerable. They can only be killed by finishing your turn next to the Colosshell assigned to you. Note that any Colosshells summoned from round six on will be vulnerable and killable like normal.
----
Bravery Expedition

The Colosshells have +50% Vitality and share their damage with each other. Dantinea also infinitely gains +100 Power at the start of her turn.
• Note that, due to the damage sharing abilities, it is possible for Dantinea to kill everybody whose Colosshell is still alive at the start of round six.

Category:Manual spells

name: Expédition de la Bravoure - Temple de Koutoulou

strategy.text:
Koutoulou Larva is not invulnerable, but it does have a passive with crucial effects:
• Any damage dealt to an enemy will make you swap places with the nearest entity (excluding summons)
• This can not be avoided with any states and is forced no matter what. Indirect damage such as poison does not trigger this mechanic.
• Doing it twice to the same enemy in the same turn will make that enemy Invulnerable and add a stack to the Madness state
• Doing it twice to the same ally will add a stack of Madness to both of you
• Koutoulou Larva can teleport across the map to any player in line of sight. In melee range it can cast Koutonian Blow, which deals quite some damage, as well as adding to the stack of Madness.
• It is crucial to try to not end your turn in its line of sight. 
• Koutoulou Larva can also swap positions with its allies twice a turn.

Madness stacks and effects:
Note that these are accumulative. Every stack has all the effects that came before it, as well as the effect of its own number.
• Stack 2+: 100% damage copied to allies within 2 cells of you
• Stack 5+: Heals received x50%
• Stack 6+: Damage sustained x100%
• Stack 10: Instant death

Every passing turn will reduce a character's stack by 1.

Most monsters in the fight have the ability to create a clone of characters. Every clone will rush towards the enemy. Damage dealt to a clone is dealt to the character, as well. 
• To kill the clone, it has to be pushed into an enemy. This will deal no damage to the 'owne

• After the third turn, they die

Each turn:
• Captain Meno will summon 3 additional allies (or more, if there are 5 or more players in the fight), so by the third turn you can expect to have a total of 9 additional enemies on the map at any given time.
• Captain Meno will apply a pink glyph under each player character or player character's summon. 
• Any player who is on a glyph at the start of '''Meno's turn (not counting the turn he creates the glyph) gains 1,000 shield points for 2 turns. 
• They will also deal 500-1000 fire damage to any linear ally (player and summon).
• Captain Meno will heal himself for a significant amount, upwards of 3,000 Vitality.
• Player characters will gain +20%''' final damage. This effect stacks and is critical to the eventual defeat of the boss.

aliases: Bravery Expedition – Belly of the Whale

strategy.text:
Protoz'orror is Invulnerable. Throughout the fight, it is Unmovable.
• The only way to move it, is by damaging it while standing linear to it. That will attract Protoz'orror in melee range. 
• This effect only activates on the very first hit received after its turn.
• Protoz'orror can be attracted linearly until the first non-linear hit.

Each time a character is hit in the fight, a purple glyph is generated on the cell they are standing on for 1 turn. Glyphs can be stacked and will separately deal the damage.
• Entering the glyph in any way will deal Air damage. 
• It is possible to generate a glyph on your own by hitting yourself on the desired cell.
• The glyph will stop any type of movement.

To make Protoz'orror vulnerable, it has to have been in the Infection state for 10 turns in a row. This state is applied when it is attracted into a purple glyph. This state will last for three turns, meaning it will need to be put in a purple glyph every second turn.
• It is possible to attract him three turns later, so long as the last character before Protoz'orror's turn moves it into the glyph.
• If the effect is lost at any time, it will have to be re-applied and the counter restarts.

While under the Infection state, Protoz'orror will turn purple, gain 2 MP, and change the properties of all its spells. It loses all its Power and base damage buffs.

Some notes:
• Protoz'orror can only hit in linear range
• Monsters in the room will summon a Malamoeba. This has to be killed every

name: Expédition de la Bravoure - Aquadôme de Merkator

strategy.text:
Merkator and all monsters in the fight are Unmovable. At the start of their turn, every monster casts Sea Yousoun, which has three effects:
• Characters and/or summons in melee range of the monster are instantly killed
• Merkator does not have this effect
• Enemies in melee range receive around 700 water damage and enter the Small Fry state for 2 turns
• All entities at exactly 2 cells away from the monster are healed by 20% of their max HP

Any attempt to remove MP from enemies inflicts 500 Fire steal.

Moving enemies is not necessary for the fight, but if need be:
• Hitting a monster in melee will pushback all entities linear and diagonally to that monster
• Hitting a monster in the Small Fry state in melee will attract all linear and diagonal instead
----
Guild Expedition:
Daring Expedition

Excluding Merkator, all monsters in the fight share damage. Every time a monster suffers damage, it and the character who dealt it will infinitely gain 2% final Damage.
----
Bravery Expedition

Merkator is Invulnerable. Every time he is made vulnerable, he infinitely gains 1 MP.
• There are two ways to make him vulnerable until the start of his next turn, which will require moving him. That can be done through the Sea An Enemy effect:
• He must be in a diagonal of a 3x3 diagonal cross within a character and take damage from a weapon
• He must be linear within a 3x3 diagonal cross around a monster, and the monster takes melee damage
Category:Manual spells
Category:Sunktuary

name: Expédition de la Bravoure - Donjon du Comte Harebourg

strategy.text:
Count Harebourg is Invulnerable throughout the fight.

At the start of your turn, you will be assigned a rotation degree. 
• Every single spell you want to aim at a specific cell, will instead have to be aimed to your rotation cell in order to be cast in the desired cell. 
• If you hit a monster while next to it, each time you hit it you will have to rotate your attacks an additional 90 degrees anti-clockwise from the original position

Once you get the rotations down, here's the easiest strategy for the fight (for 4 loot):
Save Count Harebourg for last, and don't pay too much attention to him. He has lots of range, lots of MP, and no LOS required. He will most likely always be able to hit you. Throw him away if he gets close to you, but don't go out of your way to do so.
• Cycloid
• Locks you and unmovable.
• Treadfast 
• Moves characters around and can mess up positions. Steals HP from characters and enemies in range (though not in close combat) time you hit him. The stealing is unbewitchable.
• Nocturnowl
• Latches onto you and attracts himself (+ applies the Unhealable state) if you are linear. He only has 3 MP. Hitting him in close combat will reflect all damage.
• Bubotron
• Applies spells throughout the fight, but is otherwise passive unless close to a person. At low HP it hits very highly.
• Count Harebourg
• Places a glyph around him every turn. This glyph has a range of 3 linear to where he starts his turn. If you start your turn on it (not end), you die. Do not end

name: Expédition de la Bravoure - Forgefroide de Missiz Frizz

strategy.text:
Missiz Freezz, as well as all the monsters in the room, are Invulnerable and Heavy.

At the start of a character's turn, a black 'repelling' trap of 1-cell wide cross is placed underneath that character, with the character in the centre. Summons do not create traps. When activated, the following effects happen:
• Heals the character for 15% of their maximum HP
• Pushes the character back 1 cell
• Pushes back all entities linear (up to 6 cells) and diagonal (up to 3 cells) to the character away by one cell. This removes the Invulnerable state for one turn.
• The trap disappears
• If invisible, reveal the character
To make a monster vulnerable, they will have to be pushed back through this mechanic. They do not have to move for this mechanic. They can be made vulnerable even when already against a wall.

Any pushback damage done to a character will deal 600-800 Neutral damage and 20%Erosion per damage taken. Once Missiz Freezz is dead, the neutral damage will disappear, but the erosion remains.

Traps stay on the field until they are activated. Pushback can stack if a character hasn't moved and not activated their previous trap. A character is pushed an X amount of cells depending on how many traps are activated.
• Monsters can activate these traps and receive the 15% heals, as well as creating secondary pushback effects that could deal massive amount of pushback damage to characters.

When taking damage, Missiz Freezz can cast a spell that attracts all entities within 6 range

name: Expédition de la Bravoure - Salons privés de Klime

strategy.text:
• Klime starts Invulnerable. 
• To make him vulnerable, push an ally, monster, or summon into him. This invulnerability can not be removed until the start of turn 3.
• Through the Carcassacre effect, Klime and the monsters generate a white glyph on the cell they started their turn. These glyphs last for 2 turns, and a monster finishing its turn on a glyph gains 300 damage for 1 turn.
• All characters must end their turn on a white glyph, or Klime will put them in a Pacifist state for 1 turn.
• It is possible to delay this mechanic by killing all monsters before their turn.
----
Guild Expedition:
Daring Expedition

When an enemy takes damage, they swap places with their attacker and gain 5% final damage for the rest of the fight. This can only be triggered 10 times per turn per enemy.
----
Bravery Expedition

If failing to end a turn in the white glyph, Klime will now instantly kill the character at the start of their next turn.
Category:Manual spells

strategy.text:
Kolosso is Invulnerable and accompanied by Professor Xa, who is vulnerable from the start. 
• To make Kolosso vulnerable, he has to be hit by a summon. That will make him vulnerable for one turn.

At the start of the fight, Professor Xa casts Telepathy, which deals -10,000 Heals for everybody during the fight. Lifesteal spells are unaffected. Every two turns, he puts the Morvele state on all monsters. When a monster in that state is hit, they will gain +100 damage for one turn, but also heal all characters in a 5-cell circle by 90-140.

He is also able to revive the last killed monster. They are revived at 25% HP (40% when critical hit). This can only be done in melee range, so a viable strategy is to surround him with entities.

When Professor Xa is killed, all monsters are fully healed and gain an infinite +500 Power.

strategy.text:
Celestial Bearbarian is Invulnerable, and him and all his allies are Heavy throughout the fight.
• To make him vulnerable, a character or summon has to attack him in melee range. This will make him vulnerable until the end of that character or summon's turn.
• When hitting him in melee range, any characters linear in a cross to him will be pushed back by 4 cells.
• Stealing MP, Range, or manipulating Celestial Bearbarian's position in any way, puts him in the Beark to Life state and allows him to revive up to four of his allies.

In this room, any pushback damage will instantly kill you, no matter the source. Likewise, failing to dodge an enemy that has the Catchup effect will kill all characters adjacent to that monster.

Note that if damage is being intercepted by one of the monsters, it will not trigger Celestial Bearbarian's vulnerability mechanic.

Category:Manual spells

name: Donjon de la mine de Sakaï

strategy.text:
Grohlum begins the fight being invulnerable. To make him vulnerable, any of his summons ('zombies') under the Hoarfroast effect needs to be killed right next to Grohlum. He will immediately turn vulnerable, until after the next turn of the character that made him vulnerable.
• If the summon does not yet have the correct effect, put it in Grohlum's line of sight so he can cast the correct spell on it. When cast, a helix animation should play.

Do not try to remove MP from Grohlum, as that will give him +8000 multi-elemental resistance for 1 turn, making him practically invulnerable.
----
Guild Expedition:
Daring Expedition

At the start of every character's turns, a Gobshell will be summoned linear 2 cells away from them. It is unmovable and will take its turn immediately after the character. During its turn:
• If an enemy is in line: -20% resistances (infinite)
• If a character is in line: Instantly kills them
It is important to position accordingly, since once a Gobshell has been summoned, it can not be moved. It does not require line of sight, and its range is infinite. It can be killed, however. Doing so will apply both of its effects instantly upon death (linear, infinite range, no line of sight), which can be used to debuff other monsters or get rid of summons.

Note: Since the Gobshell spawns at the start of a character's turn, that character is still able to move any other in-line characters to keep them safe.

Once Grohlum is dead, no more Gobshells will be spawned. I

strategy.text:
Shadow is Invulnerable and Heavy. At the start of his turn, he will summon his Globilum ( Invulnerable and Unmovable).
• Globilum summons Silhouette, and casts a white, 3-cell circle glyph.
• Enemies within this glyph gain 20% damage while inside it
• Silhouette is Heavy.

To make Shadow vulnerable, Silhouette has to be moved into the glyph Globilum spawns.
• If Shadow is in the glyph, the mechanic won't trigger. Only Silhouette must be inside it.

• -3 AP, 40% Erosion (for 1 turn)
Once Shadow is vulnerable, he boosts himself and his summons:
• 4 MP, 400 Power (for 1 turn)

strategy.text:
Count Razof starts the fight with over 100% in each resistance.

At the start of each turn, Count Razof will lose 20% in all resistances for each summon that is alive.

At the start of the fight, one character will randomly summon a Minikron. The summon is invulnerable, but can be instantly killed by the boss on melee. If the Minikron dies, every character in the fight will receive a 20% erosion debuff and will permanently lose 2AP for the rest of the fight.

The boss can be locked away from the Minikron and summons while the other enemies are finished off.
Category:Manual spells

strategy.text:
• Due to the highly mobile nature of the boss including her MP steal, keeping far away from and dealing with the other monsters first make the fight more manageable.
• MP reduction is extremely useful since pushing will give +% resistances and she is in the Heavy state for the duration of the fight.
• Bring a character who can apply the Gravity state to prevent from teleporting and swapping.
• While summons can allow the monsters to increase their Impatience stacks, they are also great fodder for once she has access to the instant kill spell Indecency.
• Unless you can effectively reduce MP or you are going for the Last achievement, killing first will make the fight much easier.

Category:Manual spells

strategy.text:
Every four turns, Qu'Tan will spawn one of three types of Dopples. The strategy to make Qu'Tan vulnerable will depend on the type of Dopple he summons:
• Imptin (Ecaflip) summoned in 20% of cases
• Put the Dopple next to Qu'Tan and kill it next to him.
• NOTE: It is possible that the person killing the Dopple will need to be standing right next to the Dopple to validate the kill.
• Rackimp (Osamodas) summoned in 40% of cases
• The Dopple will put the Commotion state on an ally at random. This ally and Qu'Tan have to start and end their turn right next to each other.
• Juss Imperlaik (Feca) summoned in 40% of cases
• Push the Dopple into an ally (minimum of 1 cell between ally and Dopple). This will put said ally in the Insync state. That ally should then place themselves next to Qu'Tan, where another ally will hit them with a weapon in close combat. To be clear, the ally in the Insync state and the ally putting weapon damage will have to be standing right next to each other, and the Insync ally will have to stand right next to Qu'Tan.

NOTE: This Dopple will kill itself after three turns. If you kill it before (or its three turns have passed) it will one-shot everybody within a 3 cell radius of itself. Only one person can have the Insync state at a time, if the person with the Insync state pushes the dopple into another ally, the new ally will gain the Insync state and the first ally will lose it.

strategy.text:
At the start of each turn, the enters the state "Kraken" which activates other states to prevent any movement spells (such as Cooperation, Transposition, Swap, Teleportation, Flight, Feline's Leap and Jump) as well as Bribery.

When you hit the with an elemental attack, it summons a Tentacle corresponding to that element. Each of which has a different attack:
• Water: Fourth Tentacle, which has 0% earth resistance. It causes players to skip a turn, in an AoE.
• Fire: Third Tentacle, which has 0% fire resistance. It deals large amounts of air damage.
• Earth: Second Tentacle, which has 0% water resistance. It causes a poisoning effect (ignorable).
• Air: First Tentacle, which has 0% air resistance. Its melee attack can instant-kill to a target. It also casts Natural Motivation, which gives it AP.

You must summon the Tentacles in reverse number order (from Fourth to First).

It only summons one tentacle per turn, so only hit the corresponding element of the tentacle you want summoned that turn.

It's best to place the First tentacle directly in front of the while taking its MP, with all players behind it. This blocks the 's LoS.

The other three Tentacles should be kept away, placing them around something like a Pandawasta is an option. Both to prevent their movement and as they'll need to hit it later. You might also need to take their MP to stop them moving.

Once you've set things up you can work on weakening . In order to weaken Kralove you must:
• Feed the First Tentacle

strategy.text:
Toxoliath is not invulnerable. Its mechanics lie in the following:
• At the start of the fight it casts Toxmosis. This gives all characters a very powerful poison where any MP used will deal around 700 Water Damage at the end of the character's turn, for two turns. This can instantly kill unsuspecting characters.
• Any character intending on damaging Toxoliath in their turn should not use any MP for that whole turn. 
• This effect is unbewitchable, but will return on Toxoliath's next turn.
• Once Toxoliath is dead, its effect vanishes.

For the rest of the fight, there are only a few things to keep in mind:
• Toxoliath's spell Saving Venom will randomly be cast upon a character for 1-2 turns. They will take 150% extra damage and also heal any of the enemies attacking them by 150% of damage dealt. 
• Sculucanus reflect 50% of damage done to them. To negate this effect, deal pushback damage to them. This will render it Unmovable for one turn, but also remove the reflect for one turn.
• Scoliopode can instantly kill any players in melee range.
• Most monsters inflict some type of poison relating to AP or MP usage. Check the active effects to ensure you won't die.
----
Guild Expedition:
Daring expedition

Toxoliath's powerful MP poison now counts for every monster. 
• Any character intending on damaging a monster should not move at all in their turn, or use as little MP as possible. 
• Every 1 MP = 700 Water damage at the end of their turn.

When dealing damage, characters return

strategy.text:
Tengu Snowfoux is invulnerable to any type of damage. To switch off that you need to do two things
• Push a Yokai Snowfoux diagonal to him. This puts Tengu in the Furibund state. AND
• Place a Yomi Snowfoux linear to it, then hit the Yomi with a weapon. This puts Tengu in Fulguration state. It doesn't matter how far they are from each other, they just need to be in the right spots and in the right state. The two states do not have to be activated by the same character, but they need to be activated in the same turn.
• Alternatively, you may push the Tengu Snowfoux to a square where he meets the above conditions. In this case, you should strike the Yomi Snowfoux on the same turn or one of these three monsters may move out of position on the next turn and foil your plan.

The Yokai, Yomi and Tengu are in heavy state, so they cannot be lifted or thrown. They can be pushed. If you push a Yokai or use a weapon on a Yomi, they will change appearance. They return to their original appearance after taking their turns. The state on Tengu, however, will stay until his next turn.

If you do not do both steps listed above, then this will unlock new spells for Tengu. If you only activate the Yomi Snowfoux's state, Tengu will cast a K.O. spell on its next turn (ranged, no LoS required, up to two chars a turn). If you only activate the Yokai Snowfoux's state, Tengu will cast a 100% permanent damage taken spell on all characters. 
If, however, you manage to do both steps before the Tengu's n

strategy.text:
XLII summons his Aurour. It is Unmovable and Invulnerable.

At the start of every character's turn, Aurour will move across the map in a clockwise pattern. Every position it teleports to corresponds to the hours of a clock. It stars at the 12th hour, and after every 11th hour, the cycle resets back to 12th. This continues until XLII is dead, after which the Aurour will disappear and damage will no longer be modified. 

The damage modifier counts for both monsters and characters, and thus globally affect damage received and dealt. The hour is shown by Roman Numerals above the Aurour.
• 12th hour - -60% damage
• 1st hour - -50% damage
• 2nd hour - -40% damage
• 3rd hour - -30% damage
• 4th hour - -20% damage
• 5th hour - -10% damage
• 6th hour - No modifier
• 7th hour - +10% damage
• 8th hour - +20% damage
• 9th hour - +30% damage
• 10th hour - +40% damage
• 11th hour - +50% damage (The cycle resets after this hour.)
Hitting the Aurour will teleport the character symmetrically around it. If the Aurour lands on an ally or enemy, it will swap places with them.
----

strategy.text:
Since Kimbo has a 2 cell pushback you should make sure that all players could either be pushed 2 cells or not pushed at all ''(don't stay one cell away from any obstacle).

Since Disciple pushes away entities within 2 cells from him with 3 cell pushback its better to have nothing near him.

Once all players are positioned on the correct tiles, hit Kimbo with one of the elements you chose to go with (Earth/Water or Fire/Air). At the beginning of its turn, Disciple will cast either Odd Glyph (if you hit with Earth or Water) or Even Glyph (if you hit with Fire or Air)'', which places down a chess pattern glyph. Neutral damage will not trigger either of the glyphs. 

TL;DR: 
• Fire/Air = Stand an odd number of cells away from Kimbo's summon. 
• Water/Earth = Stand an even number of cells away from Kimbo's summon. 

Once the Glyph has been cast, you need to move Kimbo onto it to weaken him. When any monster starts their turn on a glyph they gain 400% weakness in corresponding elements, and 800% resistance to the other two. 

When Kimbo is weakened you should hit it as much as possible in the weakened elements only. Hitting Kimbo with opposite elements during this stage may cause unpredictable behavior of Disciple. If you can't finish him in one turn you need to repeat this process. 

At the end of the weakened turn, Kimbo will regain his resistance and Disciple will place the glyph again (if you have hit Kimbo at least once). But be careful: Disciple may have moved since its last

strategy.text:
Minotoror has no mechanics and can be freely damaged and killed.
----
Guild Expedition:
Normal Expedition

Minotoror has 100% resistance in all elements. 
• To remove the resistances, characters need to be linear to him and damage him. This will attract the attacker, inflicting around 900 Neutral damage, and push away the target by 2 cells. Minotoror will also lose -10% resistance, but gain +10% damage for two turns.
• This effect can only be triggered 10 times per turn. Any hit to Minotoror, regardless of being linear to him, counts as one.

strategy.text:
At the start of the fight the casts Obligation, which makes it immune to all damage.

In order to make vulnerable, you need to place an ally next to it, then deal pushback damage to the ally (Summons do not work, but Sidekicks do). This makes the vulnerable for three turns and boosts its damages. Note that the ally does not need to be pushed into the specifically. It is recommended to make Obsidemon vulnerable immediately and then kill it last, as it will do zero damage if vulnerable and untouched (even with the damage buff from Obligation).

 also summons Maglobs. A summoned Maglob explodes on its fifth turn casting Shutout, dealing huge damage to, usually killing, everyone more than 7 cells away from it. Another Maglob is summoned on the turn following Shutout. So Maglobs will explode on turns 5, 10, 15 and so on (assuming isn't prevented from summoning it when it should by bribery, AP Loss etc). Whenever attacked, the summon reflects 100 dmg to anyone in 5 range circle.
Category:Manual spells

strategy.text:
Black Rat has no mechanics and can be freely damaged and killed.

Do note that through the KilleRat spell, it is possible to be instantly killed when Black Rat is in melee range of a character.
----
Guild Expedition:
Normal Expedition

There are no invulnerability mechanics to this expedition. The danger lies in the massive amounts of damage done by enemies. The following effects from Black Rat need to be considered:
• Applying two Air poisons that last for three turns to two enemies. The poison can stack, but is removed when that character is healed.
• Teleporting to a target and applying Unhealable for two turns.
• Increasing damage taken by 150%.
• Placing a black glyph of a 3-cell circle that attracts anybody entering its centre.

Category:Manual spells

strategy.text:
At the start of of the fight, enters the state Invulnerable, making him immune to all forms of damage. It also enters the state Heavy, making it immune to Karcham.

In order to remove the state Invulnerable, you must get within the movement range of a Hamrack. Hamrack will then move next to and cast Rudder, killing itself and dealing 200~600 damage to anyone within 2 cells of , and making vulnerable. You then have four turns in which to damage (if you do not kill it then you must repeat the process). Hamrack will get 3 MP when his MP gets reducted.

If you do not get Buck Anear within Hamrack's movement range, Hamrack will cast Heel at the start of its third turn, killing itself and damaging any player adjacent to it.
Category:Manual spells

strategy.text:
At the start of the fight, Drhossil casts Drhell for Glory. This causes 3 effects:
• Abyss - Triggered by Drhossil every 4 turns. 
• Instantly kills all players within 4 cells of Drhossil. 
• Alignment - Triggered at the end of each player's turn. 
• Heals all allies and enemies within 4 cells 
• -1 MP from all enemies further than 8 cells.
• Glyphyphy - Triggered whenever player is pushed or pulled. 
• Player lays a red glyph (size of 1 cell) at the square they end up at. Any entity remaining on the glyph takes x130% damage. 
• Each glyph stays on the field for 2 turns.

Most important during the fight is to avoid Drhossil's Abyss spell, cast every four turns. Drhossil has a number of high-damaging, mid-range spells and should be focused on by your team first. You can use both the glyphyphy and alignment effects to keep your team healed and keep the additional monsters away during the fight. 

Once Drhossil is dead, your biggest threat is the damage reduction from the Digshots and Dhreamers, as well as the high damaging and highly maneuverable Powa Drhells and Perfidhius.
----
Guild Expedition:
Bravery Expedition

At the start of Drhossil's turn, for every monster still alive: 
• 20 Lock and 1 MP
Drhossil starts the fight Invulnerable. To make him vulnerable:
• Kill all other monsters. Drhossil will turn vulnerable until the next wave spawns, after which he will turn Invulnerable again and also lose the accumulated Lock and MP buffs.
----

strategy.text:
Founoroshi and his allies start the fight in an invulnerable state. Founoroshi has 8 Flare summons for the duration of the fight, and each Flare has a glyph next to it.

Each turn Founoroshi will summon between 1 and 4 blue water glyphs on the map. Standing on a water glyph puts an effect on the player. 

A player or summon with a water glyph effect can stand on a Flare glyph to put the Flare out. Once all 8 Flares are out, at the start of the round the invulnerable state is deactivated on Founoroshi and his allies.

The Flares will relight after 7 turns of being put out. If all Flares are lit, Founoroshi will instantly kill all players on his next turn. On the first turn you must put out one Flare or Founoroshi will instantly kill the entire team on his next turn.

There are also Barrels near the Flares. Hitting a Barrel will heal and shield you. If you are away from the barrel you hit, it will pull you in 1 cell. If you are next to the barrel you hit, it will push you away.

is Invulnerable and cannot be made vulnerable. Moving in any direction while it has the elemental state will summon the Nugget of the same element 2 cells away from it. You only need to kill 1 Nugget of each element, and they will only be summoned in that specific order, meaning you can only get Fire Nuggets until 1 is killed, and so on. will move players around and try to lock them, making it difficult to attack the Nuggets properly. Think of its Nuggets as pieces of it falling off which you must "eat" before it has a chance to merge back together with them. You may notice that shrinks as you "devour" each of the Nuggets.

The Nuggets are vulnerable to their related element while resistant to all the other elements. Additionally, the Nuggets are very resistant to ranged damage, although their resistance to ranged damage is reduced as you move through the order of elements, meaning Fire is the most resistant to ranged damage and Earth is the least resistant to ranged damage, with 100%, 70%, 40% and 10% ranged resistance for the Fire, Air, Water and Earth Nuggets respectively, so melee damage is generally more preferable. The Nugge
