strategy.text:
Initially, while not compulsory, it's highly recommended you learn Bow (spell) spell to have more control over the fight. It allows you to control a kwapa, thus allowing you to position Water Cucumbomb properly to reach Nagate. Lowering kwapa's HP as much as possible before casting bow also seems to increase chances of being able to control said kwapa. 

At the beginning of the fight, Nagate will teleport to the top of the map and make herself invulnerable to ranged damage. You'll have to make your way to her little island in order to kill her.

Leave the enemies alive and wait until they summon a Water Cucumbomb. If you have bow spell, leave them alive just until you take control of one or kill all but one to take control (Kwakumber being best candidate, since it's rather inofensive). It looks a bit different than the normal bombs (it won't have a glyph too). It seems to be random how often a Water Cucumbomb will be summoned as oppose to a normal one, but the chance is small. Don't be surprised if you wait 15 turns or more without Bow (spell).

Once a Water Cucumbomb is spawned, move it to the wall nearest Nagate and stand next to it (to direction of Nagate). Then hit that Water Cucumbomb on melee range without killing it. You will be teleported to the top side of the map. If you don't end your turn in melee with Nagate, she will teleport you back down to the other side of the map on her turn.

Characters are teleported back down to the bottom side of the map when Nagate die

strategy.text:
The Tanukouï San has 3 phases with health thresholds (recognizable by the Roman numeral I, II, or III above its head) and X.5 phases for each phase which, if you do not pass it in the phase next before the start of his next tu turn, the boss will heal 1xxx (20% HP approx max) at the beginning of his turn.

Here is the list of the different phases of Tanukouï San (with a 4 loot fight):
• Threshold 1: Full HP.
• Threshold 1.5: 4575 HP (healing of 1xxx HP if you do not pass it in phase 2).
• Threshold 2: 4200 HP (approximately).
• Threshold 2.5: 2575 HP (approximately) (healing of 1xxx hp if you do not pass it to phase 3).
• Threshold 3: 2200 HP (approximately).
• Threshold 3.5: 800 HP (approximately) (healing of 1xxx hp if you do not kill him).

Like the monsters in the dungeon, the Tanukouï San also has a geoglyph but it is scalable! Indeed its glyph will change each time it is in one of its three phases. Thus its geoglyph will have a different shape if it is in phase I, in phase II or in phase III:
• In phase I: Its geoglyph is a square with a side 7x7.
• In phase II: Its geoglyph is an even checkerboard of radius 6.
• In phase III: Its glyph is an infinite star ( infinite line and diagonal).

Placement classes (Pandawa, Cra, Eliotrope, Xelor, etc…) are recommended for this dungeon in order to easily remove the Invulnerable state from monsters and the Boss.

For this fight, move the Tanukouï San and the monsters off the geoglyphs in order to remove their Invulnerable state. T

Guild Expedition:
Normal Expedition

Minotoror has 100% resistance in all elements.

• This effect can only be triggered 10 times per turn. Any hit to Minotoror, regardless of being linear to him, counts as one.

strategy.text:
Crocabulia has no mechanics and can be freely damaged and killed.
----
Guild Expedition:
Normal Expedition

Crocabulia is Invulnerable. Every turn she will summon a Shell, up to 10 cells away. This has 55,000 HP and 8MP, but there is no need to kill it. 

At the start of Crocabulias turn, she will instantly kill all summoned Shells that are linear and diagonal from her at an infinite range. Any Shells within 3 cells of an exploding one will also explode.

Any entities (monsters AND players) within 3 cells of a Shell when it explodes will experience the following:
• Monsters are fully healed
• Characters or summons are instantly killed 

To make Crocabulia vulnerable, she needs to have exploded three shells directly by herself. Chained explosions do not count.

This means at least three Shells must have been put in linear or diagonal lines from her and been exploded by her start-of-turn mechanic. Note that the Shells do not have to have been killed within the same turn. As long as she's exploded at least three during the fight, she will become vulnerable.

strategy.text:
Royal Tofu has no mechanics and can be freely damaged and killed.

----
Guild Expedition:
Normal expedition

Royal Tofu is Invulnerable. 
To make it vulnerable, it must not use any MP for two turns in a row. This can be done in two ways:
• Lock or trap Royal Tofu so it can not move.
• Every time any monster is hit in melee range, it will permanently remove 1 MP from it for the rest of the fight. This can be done 5 times per turn. When the MP is low enough, it can be safely locked, or its MP can be reduced to 0 to prevent its moving at all.

Every two turns, all monsters receive a buff that lasts for 1 turn:
• Turn 2 - +50% Final Damage.
• Turn 4 - Royal Tofu receives shield points equal to 10% of its HP.
• Turn 6 - Invulnerable for one turn.
• Turn 8 - Heals for 10% of its HP.
• Turn 10 - Repeats the buffs starting with Turn 2.

Every turn, Royal Tofu places a black glyph of a 4-cell circle that lasts two turns. For monsters, this glyph reduces ranged damage taken by -20%. It also heals them for 8% of their maximum HP.

strategy.text:
At the beginning of the fight Fraktal will summon unmovable and invulnerable Aurour. 

Fraktal's room resembles a clock and at the start of every player’s turn Aurour will be moving across it like a clock hand. Depending on '''Fraktal's HP and distance between Fraktal and Aurour it will go in one of directions:
• Clockwise fight continues as normal.
• Counter-clockwise all damage on the map is converted to healing.

Thus fight becomes about making the clock turn clockwise. There are two situations when Aurour moves clockwise:
• Fraktal has over 50% HP and Aurour is 5 cells away or more
• Fraktal has under 50% HP the Aurour is 4 cells away or less

Otherwise it will turn counter-clockwise.'''

If it did move counter-clockwise you can heal an ally with damage, but you won't be able to damage any monsters. You will need to keep Fraktal close to her Aurour while she has under 50% HP to continue hurting her until she dies, and when she does her Aurour will die with her. 

Note that whenever Fraktal is moved she gains 1 MP for 1 turn.
Category:Divine Dimensions
Category:Manual spells

Black Rat has no mechanics and can be freely damaged and killed.

Do note that through the KilleRat spell, it is possible to be instantly killed when Black Rat is in melee range of a character.
----
Guild Expedition:
Normal Expedition

There are no invulnerability mechanics to this expedition. The danger lies in the massive amounts of damage done by enemies. The following effects from Black Rat need to be considered:

• Teleporting to a target and applying Unhealable for two turns.
• Increasing damage taken by 150%.
• Placing a black glyph of a 3-cell circle that attracts anybody entering its centre.

strategy.text:
Colousses by default reflects 90% of damage (except for poisons). 

Colousses has 3 summons, each in state either I, II or III. These states change randomly at start of Colousses's turn. Boss' damage reflect is removed when all summons are in same matching state, but only if you made them that way. 

You can change the states by hitting one of these summons in melee, which will also push you back 4 cells. Doing this will add +I to the state of that summon and add +II to other summons. 

The number of possible states is three so adding +I to a III state loops it back into I.

Situation 1. Two summons have matching states:
Hit non matching summon until it matches (most common)
• Non matching summon lacks I. Hit it 2 times
• Non matching summon lacks II. Hit it 1 time
• Non matching summon is over by I. Hit it 1 time
• Non matching summon is over by II. Hit it 2 times

Situation 2. No summons have matching states:
Hit any one of them 1 time and whoever is left non matching hit 2 times (or just wait for easier turn)

strategy.text:
At the start of his turn, Dragon Pig will cast Lethal Blunder. This end-of-turn poison lasts for two turns, and deals 2400 Neutral damage per AP the player has spent in their turn. Resistances do not affect this damage in any way.

Any player that starts round one before Dragon Pig is able to damage enemies freely. Any players that start after Dragon Pig should use no AP if possible. If going for the challenges, try to use as little as possible to get next to enemies for the Impertinence challenge.
----
Guild Expedition:
Normal Expedition

Dragon Pig's expedition is a wave fight.
• Wave 1: 3 Gorgoyles
• Gorgoyles will spawn a Farle's Pig at the end of their turn. 
• It can kill itself to deal up to 50% of their HP in damage to all characters in the fight. This can only be cast in melee range, however, and only 3 rounds after it was summoned.
• Every Gorgoyle defeated will give a bonus, depending on the type of Gorgoyle, as well as a generic bonus. By the end of the fight all players (and their summons) have a permanent bonus of +3 AP, +4 MP, and +6 Range, as well as:
• Muddy: +20% resistance (infinite) and 4,000 shields every 3 rounds.
• Lazy: 500% pushback resistance and 10% of max HP heals every 2 rounds
• Gluttonous: 200% Vitality and 200% final Damage (infinite)
• Wave 2: Dragon Pig himself, as well as 3 Dorgan Ation and 2 Pignolia
Category:Manual spells

strategy.text:
Moowolf is Invulnerable. To make him vulnerable, characters/summons need to hit him once with a damage spell. This will make him vulnerable until the end of their turn. Summons will need to hit him separately from their character.

Each attack against the monsters (excluding their summons) causes them to gain 30% power for four turns.
----
Guild Expedition:
Normal Expedition

Moowolf is the only monster in the fight. He is not invulnerable, but he he has 30,000 HP and 10 MP, as well as 200 MP Parry. He has several effects in the fight:
• Any character in melee range at the start of his turn will be instantly killed.
• Any character or summon killed by this mechanic will change into a Boowolf. Two can be summoned per turn.
• From turn three onwards, every three turns, he kills all Boowolf and heals for 1,000 HP and gains 10% damage for two turns. 
• If there are no Boowolf present, he loses -30% resistance for two turns instead.
Category:Mine:-2,2
Category:Manual spells

strategy.text:
Koolich has no mechanics and can be freely damaged and killed.

Guild Expedition:
Normal Expedition

Koolich has 100% resistances in all elements. Every four turns he can turn invisible.

Damaging Koolich will turn entities (both monsters and characters) into Gobkools when:
• On even turns they are linear with Koolich 
• On odd turns they are diagonal with Koolich
This effect lasts for one turn. 

If Koolich suffers more damage, any entities already in the Gobkool state in-line with him (linear on even turns, diagonal on odd), will instantly die.
• Any Cave Gobballs in-line with Koolich will not die, but instead gain +3AP and MP, +100% final Damage, 10% of its maximum HP healed per turn, as well as gaining a new spell.

To make Koolich vulnerable:
• Any Gobkool (whether a player character or a summon) starting its turn within 2 cells of a monster removes 20% resistance to that monster for two turns.
• Multiple Gobkool's will need to be placed within 2 cells of Koolich to enable a substantial amount of damage to be done. Keep in mind that transformed characters will instantly die if they are still in-line with Koolich when he takes damage.
Category:Manual spells

strategy.text:
At the start of the fight, Brutatax casts the spell Offringold, which causes Brutatax and any character that hits her to gain 100% power, infinitely stackable. When Brutatax reaches half health, every hit on her will cause her to gain 500 shield points (weapons and spells with multiple damage pools witll cause her to gain multiples of 500 shield points). Between 2 and 3 turns after this, she will cast Nonoub Noskam, removing all the shields she has gained and all the power bonuses from herself and each character that hit her, followed by recasting Offringold. This will repeat as long as she is alive.

Taking her range and movement points can be useful to prevent Brutatax from dealing any damage to you and your team. Unbuffed, Brutatax deals little damage. However, she will gain a power bonus each time you hit her, so over time she can begin to deal some serious damage. When she reaches half health and begins gaining shields, use single damage pool spells and weapons to minimize the amount of shields she gains. Alternatively, use only high-base damage spells on buffed characters to deal enough damage to pierce the shields she gains or have a character that can reliably unbewitch the shield bonus.
Category:Divine Dimensions
Category:Manual spells

By default, Moon has 200% resistances in every element. To reduce the resistance, totems have to be placed on their corresponding glyphs.

At the start of Moon's turn, he will summon Darkli Moon. Darkli Moon summons one totem of a random element: Red ( Fire), blue ( Water), yellow ( Earth), or green ( Air). The element of a totem can also be noted by checking its elemental resistances: whatever it is 100% resistant to, that is the element of the totem. Every turn, Darkli Moon will swap places with one character or summon. At the end of his turn, he will summon an elemental glyph that corresponds with the existing totem, and will ends his turn standing on that glyph.
• Attacking Darkli Moon with any spell will make him and the character switch places and give Moon 100 Power.

Every five turns, Moon will kill all summoned totems and summon new ones, requiring characters to restart their attempts.

Totems can not be carried, pushed, pulled, or swapped with placement spells. Instead, they must be hit with a spell of the corresponding element by a character linear to it. This will attract the totem in melee range of the character. Totems can be moved an infinite number of times.
• Traps, glyphs, and poison diagonally from the totem will not trigger the attraction.
• Sometimes the glyphs are in positions where it is impossible to move the totem onto it. In that case it is recommended to wait for new totems.

strategy.text:
The boss gains bonus vitality, damage, and resistance% for each of its allies that is killed.

Do NOT hit the boss while standing within 2 cells of him (always be 3+ cells away when attacking the boss), failing to do this will activate Primitive Reflex (note the spell Kharnage).

Try to stay far away from it, and don't let it get linear to you or it'll pull itself to you using Collision.

The boss only has 3 MP, and not a lot of MP Parry. You can fairly easily steal all of its MP each turn if you have some MP reducing abilities.

strategy.text:
Legendary Crackler has no mechanics and can be freely damaged and killed.

----
Guild Expedition:
Normal expedition

Legendary Crackler has a buff that gives:
• +50% all resistances, +200 flat damage for all elements, and +500 AP and MP Parry.

When he is hit with a neutral element, all of its allies will gain 3,000 shield points for two turns.

NOTE: Legendary Crackler will cast Dazing Stone when it has 20 AP. This will deal massive amounts of damage, so it is recommended to prevent him from being able to cast it.

The other monsters:
• +20% all resistances, +100 flat damage for all elements, and +200 AP and MP 

At the end of every monster's turn, they place a black glyph on their cell. If a character enters this glyph, they enter the Crackling state:
• Gives 30% of your HP as shield, -100 MP, and +100% Damage received for the rest of the fight
This state allows for characters to debuff the enemies, as well as removing the state from themselves, in two ways:
• When taking melee damage, all monsters adjacent to that character lose their buff for three turns.
• When taking pushback damage, all monsters in a 3 by 3 square around that character lose their buff for two turns.

Category:Manual spells

• Depending on the monster killed, the below buff:

Smiths' Chest has no mechanics and can be freely damaged and killed.

Guild Expedition:
Normal expedition
• Smiths' Chest will remain Invulnerable until its first turn after all other monsters are killed.
• If you kill a monster, Smiths' Chest will immediately teleport next to you and instantly kill you. 
• To avoid this mechanic, keep a monster at 10% HP or less. At the start of Smiths' Chest's turn, it will kill any monster at or below that threshold.
• The same mechanic counts for player characters as well. If a player has hit that threshold, Smiths' Chest will kill them at the start of its turn.
• Every time a monster is killed, the Smiths' Chest gains +1 MP, +5% Final Damage, and visually gets 10% larger.

Category:Manual spells
